title: keraselma
intro: Keraselma, also known as Lathraea squamaria, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family that grows in Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of keraselma include relief from congestion, sore throats, and other respiratory problems, as well as its potential to help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, keraselma is typically used as a dried flower or root infusion, often in combination with other herbs, to create teas, tinctures, and tonics that promote respiratory health.
